human_verification.notes: Verified DevSecOps scope, source-backed workflow stages, authority boundaries, confidentiality handling, evaluation coverage, functionality map completeness, research summary completeness, and specification-based runtime posture for issue #1619.

review_notes: All 18 scenarios pass with explicit DevSecOps delivery, governance, evidence, confidentiality, signoff refusal, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence escalation, tenant adaptation, and no-unreviewed-regression behavior.
All required abilities across shared, compliance-controls-and-governance, and professional-and-information-services layers are fully covered.
The refreshed package exceeds the legacy minimum scenario count and preserves strict validation with a 1.00 pass rate.

specialist_owned_evidence: DevSecOps outputs are tied to named repository, pipeline, scanner, SBOM, provenance, artifact, release, policy, confidentiality, and approval records.

The lane refuses adjacent cloud, network, IAM, endpoint, production monitoring, ticketing, SOC, incident response, penetration testing, legal, certification, contractual, executive, product strategy, pricing, and editorial authority.

Confidentiality and client-delivery controls are explicit, including approved-recipient checks and restricted handling for source code, vulnerabilities, credentials, scanner results, and client commitments.
Remediation, exception escalation, risk acceptance routing, rollback conditions, and no-unreviewed-regression behavior are explicit.
Runtime remains specification-based, with no retained-implementation claim and promotion criteria documented in manifest and deployment notes.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains after constraining the lane to DevSecOps guidance and review, public secure software baselines, tenant-supplied records, and explicit escalation boundaries.
Pack is suitable for validated spec-pack deployment and can be promoted to a retained-implementation runtime only through a separate frequent-use promotion decision.
